decisions.briefing: Markets drifted slightly lower today with NASDAQ -0.4% and S&P 500 -0.1% — a classic neutral session with no clear directional catalyst. Despite having $677 in cash and 4 open slots, strategy memory is explicit: preserve dry powder through neutral days and deploy on the next genuine risk-on signal (NASDAQ +2%+). Existing positions show no stop-loss concerns, so the right move is to hold everything and wait.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities treaded water on Thursday as investors paused ahead of upcoming earnings reports and macroeconomic data, leaving the S&P 500 down just 0.1% and the NASDAQ off 0.4%. The mild weakness reflected a lack of fresh catalysts rather than active selling, with institutional money rotating quietly within sectors rather than making directional bets. Without a clear bullish or bearish driver, markets settled into a holding pattern typical of mid-earnings-season caution.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市週四陷入膠著，投資者在即將到來的企業財報及宏觀數據公布前選擇按兵不動，標普500指數微跌0.1%，納斯達克指數下跌0.4%。此次小幅回落更多反映催化劑缺失，而非主動拋售，機構資金在板塊內部悄然輪動，未有明確方向性佈局。在財報季中段，市場進入觀望模式，等待更清晰的指引。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Dry powder refers to cash or liquid assets held in reserve, ready to deploy when a clear opportunity arises. Experienced investors deliberately hold dry powder through low-conviction market days so they can act decisively when conditions favor strong entries — such as a broad risk-on rally. Deploying cash just to avoid sitting idle often leads to buying at suboptimal moments.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「乾火藥」（Dry Powder）指投資者保留的現金或流動資產，隨時準備在適當時機迅速進場。有經驗的投資者會刻意在市場方向不明時持現觀望，以便在市況明確轉強（如大盤出現風險偏好上升的走勢）時果斷出手。若僅為避免現金閒置而倉促入市，往往容易在時機欠佳時高位接貨。
